ShortComplex.QuasiIso.exact_iff transports categorical exactness; concrete MetrizableLCA exactness-to-kernel/surjectivity extraction remains missing.
Right calculus for boundedHomotopyExactWeakEquivalence is reduced to direct inverse-image transfer or localized left-adjoint/counit-membership input; product success remains false.
ShortComplex.Exact plus element/topology fields can build strictShortExact, but ShortComplex.Exact alone cannot imply target g surjectivity; product success remains false.
MetrizableLCA.strictShortExact now implies exactness of S.map MetrizableLCA.forgetToAddCommGrpCat; product success remains false.

Concrete cokernel-subgroup-top is equivalent to zero categorical cokernel object and, under open-map hypothesis, equivalent to surjectivity; CokernelTopStrictInput packages the strict-exact consumer. Product success remains false.

Concrete finite limits/products/equalizers on the opposite bounded-complex source feed conditional equalizer and finite-limit constructors on the abstract opposite localization; product success remains false.
RightRouteInput and LeftRouteInput package the exact missing Verdier comparison adjunction API needed to derive left/right calculus; product success remains false.
Concrete source finite-limit facts and finite-product/equalizer/finite-limit transfer inputs are packaged for the opposite localization route; product success remains false.
Arbitrary MetrizableLCA cokernels and finite colimits are already implemented in parent v252; later v267-v270 strengthen the right-exactness interface. No new target code needed.
Factorized adjunction and membership inputs are isolated and shown to transport to localized unit/counit membership; product success remains false.

Direct finite-product stability is available but insufficient for the opposite route; finite-coproduct stability plus formal op transport is isolated as the remaining bridge. Product success remains false.
Opposite finite-product and WalkingParallelPair stability fields are packaged and reduced to direct finite-coproduct/coequalizer closure plus op transport; product success remains false.
ShortComplex.Exact plus homology/forgetful-preservation inputs gives kernel equality; topology plus v270 cokernel/epi right inputs constructs strictShortExact. Product success remains false.

Exactness-to-kernel is available under homology/forgetful-preservation inputs, but exactness-to-Epi and quasiIso-exactness-to-Epi are formally refuted by an exact 0->0->ZMod2 target. Product success remains false.
Generic finite-coproduct stability of W now transports to finite-product stability of W.op. Direct finite-coproduct stability remains open; product success remains false.

ShortExact supplies Epi and cokernel-zero/top endpoints; right-homology zero requires a comparison to local cokernelObj, and a generic rightHomologyZero-to-Epi principle is refuted by the ZMod2 endpoint.

API scout confirmed no concrete factorized adjunction constructors beyond W260; v287 already records the checked boundary, so no separate port was needed.

Specializes objectwise WalkingParallelPair functor-category localization for the opposite bounded weak-equivalence property; two fixed-target lift/fac/uniq packages imply the localization input, with objectwise inverts and blueprint-to-lift checked.

Shows T.rightHomology is Kernel T.fromOpcycles and MetrizableLCA.cokernelπ T.g is Cokernel T.fromOpcycles; Epi T.fromOpcycles is a positive endpoint input, while rightHomology zero alone is insufficient.

Adds unconditional ContainsIdentities for boundedHomotopyExactWeakEquivalence and its opposite; records that direct boundedExactWeakEquivalence still needs identity-cone exactness or a homotopy-class finite-limit assembly.

Added source-level finite-coproduct-to-opposite-finite-product transport and concrete isStableUnderFiniteProducts_op_metrizableLCA. Opposite finite-product stability is no longer an open input.

ShortExact supplies Epi and cokernel-zero/top endpoints; right-homology zero requires a comparison to local cokernelObj, and a generic rightHomologyZero-to-Epi principle is refuted by the ZMod2 endpoint.

Flattened W257 localized comparison inputs into FactorizedRightUnitBoundary and FactorizedLeftCounitBoundary; consumers transport adjunction and unit/counit membership across verdierComparisonLocalizationIso, but concrete constructors remain missing.
Consumed in v288 opposite finite-limit assembly.
Consumed in v288 opposite finite-limit assembly.

Specializes objectwise WalkingParallelPair functor-category localization for the opposite bounded weak-equivalence property; two fixed-target lift/fac/uniq packages imply the localization input, with objectwise inverts and blueprint-to-lift checked.

Shows T.rightHomology is Kernel T.fromOpcycles and MetrizableLCA.cokernelπ T.g is Cokernel T.fromOpcycles; Epi T.fromOpcycles is a positive endpoint input, while rightHomology zero alone is insufficient.

Parent-side v292 source progress; no support worker result consumed.

W312 proves the W311 cover package follows from strict short exactness of the WPP-op colimit point by identity cover maps; remaining work is constructing that strict colimit point or direct quotient/coequalizer cover data; not product success.

W313 reduces WPP-op strictShortExact colimit closure to HasHomology, forgetful PreservesHomology, exactness, left closed embedding, and right open map; right surjectivity is supplied by categorical epi plus openness; not product success.

W286 reduces rightOpenMap WPP-op colimit closure to component X₂/X₃ colimit open-map preservation; not product success.
W287 reduces algebraic exactness at the WPP-op colimit point to ShortComplex.Exact plus HasHomology and forgetful PreservesHomology; not product success.
W315 reduces colimit-point HasHomology input to CategoryWithHomology MetrizableLCA; not product success.
W316 reduces forgetful PreservesHomology to PreservesFiniteLimits and PreservesFiniteColimits for forget₂ MetrizableLCA AddCommGrpCat; not product success.
W289 reduces leftClosedEmbedding WPP-op colimit closure to preservation of component closed embeddings under WPP-op short-complex colimits; not product success.
W317 proves exactAcyclic WPP-op colimit closure from three direct field closures: left closed embedding, right open map, and algebraic exactness; not product success.
